rationale: Completeness/retention lens (fbel.5-class check). Diff is +70/-0, pure additions; every load-bearing section confirmed RETAINED intact at 84001704. ms-panel-tally: Allow-branch artifact-gate screen (Step 1, 'HARD-blocks regardless of vote count and regardless of whether any reviewer set hard_block' + lola-f4a8 $417 postmortem), Artifact gates table, Consolidate (Step 2), After-a-halt, Abandon (§4), Escape hatch — all unchanged. ms-panel-run: Step 0, Launch the panel, Codex failure detection, Working directory matters, Slot lens defaults, Anti-patterns — all retained, only gated behind superseded callouts. Slot lens defaults correctly NOT superseded (the retained authoring step). Critically: the new Workflow-path note does NOT let a workflow panel skip the artifact-gate screen or consolidation — it explicitly reaffirms 'Artifact gates ... unchanged on both paths' and retains 'the merge terminal', which the Allow-branch screen gates as a precondition. Both mirrors byte-identical. No load-bearing weakening. One advisory below.

findings.note: The Allow-branch aggregated-CCR artifact-gate screen physically lives in Step 1, and the note phrases Step 1's workflow-path change as 'just reading that already-produced output.' Since the binary `mindspec panel tally` (which the workflow ran) only mechanizes the hard_block-flag disjunct (gate.go cases 9-10) and does NOT perform the aggregated-CCR HARD-block screen, a tighter note would name that Step-1 Allow-branch screen explicitly as still-required on the workflow path. Retention is preserved as written (via 'Artifact gates unchanged on both paths' + retained 'merge terminal'), so this is a strengthening, not a blocking weakening.

Step 0 content is fully retained (not weakened), so no retention finding. The Runner-dispatch section already directs the workflow-path reader past Step 0-through-Anti-patterns; leaving Step 0 without its own superseded callout is acceptable for the retention lens.
